info.title: SHKeeper API
info.description:
# Introduction
SHKeeper - is an open-source, self-hosted cryptocurrency payment processor. It uniquely serves as both a gateway and a merchant, enabling you to accept payments in various cryptocurrencies without fees and intermediaries.

# Authentication
You can authenticate either via Basic Auth or an API key depending from endpoint requirement. You can generate or view an API key in the SHKeeper UI under `Wallets` -> `Manage` -> `API key`, the API key is the same for each wallet. Basic HTTP credentials are your SHKeeper UI user and password. For metrics endpoints use `METRICS_USERNAME` and `METRICS_PASSWORD`, with default values `shkeeper` and `shkeeper`.

<!-- Redoc-Inject: <security-definitions> -->

# Integration Boundary
SHKeeper emits payment and payout execution facts. Customer-facing business decisions such as credit, manual review, reject, hold, withdrawal eligibility, and amount policy are made by the consuming application, not by SHKeeper. Optional AML enrichment is informational evidence for that upstream policy decision.

get.description: Get a list of cryptocurrencies available for operation from SHKeeper (these are the ones that are online and not disabled in the admin panel).

value.traceback:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/shkeeper.io/shkeeper/api_v1.py", line 71, in payment_request
    invoice = Invoice.add(crypto=crypto, request=req)

    invoice = cls.query.filter_by(external_id=request['external_id'],
KeyError: 'external_id'

post.description:
The callback is sent to the specified `callback_url` provided during invoice creation. SHKeeper can send an unconfirmed transaction callback first, and later sends a confirmed payment callback after the transaction reaches the required confirmation state. For confirmed payment callbacks, SHKeeper sends a notification for each transaction related to the invoice, even if the invoice is already in `PAID`/`OVERPAID` status. The transaction that triggered the confirmed callback is marked with `trigger=true`.

Current callbacks contain payment facts and optional AML enrichment facts only. The consuming application should decide credit/manual review/reject using its own policy and the `aml` object on the trigger transaction. Use normalized `aml.checked`, `aml.supported`, `aml.check_status`, and `aml.reason_code` for decisioning; treat `aml.provider_status` as raw provider state. Unconfirmed callbacks never include AML enrichment and should not be used as a final credit decision.

parameters.name: X-Shkeeper-Api-Key
parameters.description: HTTP header to authenticate and validate by your system.

202.description: If a payment notification is successfully processed by your server, it should return the HTTP code `202 Accepted`. Any other response or connection failure will cause SHKeeper to resend the payment notification every `60` seconds.

get.description: Get detailed information about status of created invoice in SHKeeper by external_id
Get all generated cryptocurrency addresses for specified cryptocurrency

get.summary: Get transaction for SHKeeper generated cryptocurrency address
get.description: Get incoming/deposit invoice transactions and unconfirmed deposit transactions for the specified cryptocurrency address. Outgoing operational transactions, such as wallet sweep transactions, are excluded from this address-scoped response.
Get payment info by transaction and external ID

key.description: Decryption key that was set on the first run of SHKeeper on demand

get.description: Get SHKeeper metrics for wallets status

post.description: Multipayout is not supported for the following cryptocurrencies: `XMR`, `BTC`, `LTC`, and `DOGE`. For these, you need to use the [Payout](#tag/Payouts/operation/CreatePayoutTask) method.

status.description:
Payout task completing status.
`PENDING` means the task is in progress, and no result is available at this point yet.
`SUCCESS` means the task is complete, and you are presented with the results.
`FAILURE` means the task is complete, but with some issues. Check `result` field.

value.result: Have not enough tokens on fee account, need 300.11 have 0

202.description: Execution accepted or idempotent existing execution returned

400.description: Invalid payout request or disabled/unsupported rail

401.description: Missing or unknown payout auth headers

403.description: Invalid signature, replayed nonce, expired timestamp, or forbidden rail key

409.description: The external_id already exists with a different canonical request

503.description: Enabled rail is not dispatchable or callback endpoint is not configured

post.description:
Create an idempotent service-consumer USDT payout execution.

This endpoint is a durable accept boundary. A `202` response with `state=CREATED` means SHKeeper stored the execution and will dispatch it through the payout reconciler. Consumers must use callbacks and `GET /api/v1/payout-executions/{external_id}` for monotonic progression.

The request accepts only execution-contract fields: `external_id`, `asset`, `network`, `amount`, and `destination`. Additional request fields are not part of the signed execution contract.

The request is signed with HMAC-SHA256. The signature base is `timestamp\nnonce\nMETHOD\ncanonical_path\ncanonical_query\nbody_sha256`.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Consumer
parameters.description: Configured service consumer id for payout execution.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Key-Id
parameters.description: Configured key id for the payout HMAC secret.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp in seconds. Default allowed clock skew is 300 seconds.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Nonce
parameters.description: Submit/status requests must use a one-time nonce. Callback deliveries use the callback `event_id` as the nonce; retries for the same callback reuse that nonce and must be deduplicated by `event_id`.

x-codeSamples.label: CLI
x-codeSamples.source:
curl --location --request POST 'https://demo.shkeeper.io/api/v1/payout-executions' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'X-Payout-Consumer: merchant-app' \
--header 'X-Payout-Key-Id: default' \
--header 'X-Payout-Timestamp: 1780560600' \
--header 'X-Payout-Nonce: 2c338f55-05ba-4a9c-aaf4-caa8fbd3148f' \
--header 'X-Payout-Signature: SIGNATURE_HEX' \
--data-raw '{"external_id":"W123456789","asset":"USDT","network":"TRON","amount":"25.000000","destination":"TQZL6tWjV3L1y7mK7Q9..."}'

401.description: Missing or unknown payout auth headers

403.description: Invalid signature, replayed nonce, expired timestamp, or forbidden rail key

404.description: Execution not found for the authenticated consumer

get.description:
Return payout execution status scoped by the authenticated payout consumer.

Use this endpoint after submit timeout, callback delay, scheduler reconciliation, and before any manual payout action. The response contains monotonic state evidence: `event_version`, `state_transition_id`, hashes, sidecar state, txids/message hashes, failure fields, and `reconciliation_required`.

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: Immutable consumer-side payout request id used when the payout execution was submitted.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Consumer
parameters.description: Configured service consumer id for payout execution.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Key-Id
parameters.description: Configured key id for the payout HMAC secret.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp in seconds. Default allowed clock skew is 300 seconds.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Nonce
parameters.description: Submit/status requests must use a one-time nonce. Callback deliveries use the callback `event_id` as the nonce; retries for the same callback reuse that nonce and must be deduplicated by `event_id`.

409.description: Requested resolution is not allowed from the current execution state

post.summary: Record operator manual-resolution evidence for a payout execution.
post.description:
Record operator manual-resolution evidence for a payout execution.

This endpoint is for SHKeeper operators when an execution is in a manual review boundary such as `RECONCILIATION_REQUIRED`. It records structured technical evidence, writes an audit row, and moves the execution through a constrained resolution state.

x-codeSamples.label: CLI
x-codeSamples.source:
curl --location --request POST 'https://demo.shkeeper.io/api/v1/payout-executions/123/manual-resolution' \
--header 'Authorization: Basic YOUR_BASIC_AUTH' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{"resolution_status":"SAFE_FOR_MANUAL_PAYOUT","operator_note":"negative chain evidence checked","evidence":{"network":"TRON","asset":"USDT","execution_id":123,"external_id":"W123456789","destination":"TQZL6tWjV3L1y7mK7Q9...","amount":"25.000000","last_state":"RECONCILIATION_REQUIRED","last_sidecar_state":"RECONCILIATION_REQUIRED","source_wallet":"fee_deposit","token_contract":"TRC20-USDT","checked_sources":["tron-fullnode","tron-indexer"],"searched_block_range":{"from":100,"to":200},"matching_transfer_found":false,"pending_original_artifact":false}}'

GetCryptoResponse.description: List of available for operate crypto curencies.

status.description: Status of obtaining available crypto currencies

CallbackTransaction.description: Callback transaction. Only the transaction with `trigger=true` can include AML enrichment fields and SHKeeper AML correlation identifiers.

amount_fiat_without_fee.description: Transaction amount in fiat currency without SHKeeper fee. Can be negative in case of `FIXED_FEE` or `PERCENT_OR_MINIMAL_FIXED_FEE` policy if transaction `amount_fiat` less than `fee_fixed`

fee_fiat.description: SHKeeper fee in fiat for transaction calculated according chosen policy for related cryptocurrency

trigger.description: `true` if this transaction was the trigger for the callback

deposit_id.description: SHKeeper AML deposit identifier for the trigger transaction. Present only when an AML check row exists.

idempotency_key.description: Stable idempotency key for the trigger transaction AML/deposit event, for example `USDT:<txid>:shkeeper-tx-9`. Present only when an AML check row exists.

balance_fiat.description: Balance accumulated on invoice in fiat curency

external_id.description: A unique order_id or invoice_id from your store.

fiat.description: Fiat currency code in `ISO 4217` format for conversion. Currently, only `USD` is supported.

status.description:
SHKeeper invoice status.
`UNPAID` - invoice with no payments.
`PARTIAL` - partial invoice payment.
`PAID` - full invoice payment.
`OVERPAID` - overpaid invoice.

message.description: Error message. Sending in case of error status

CreatePaymentRequest.description: Create SHKeeper invoice and obtain crypto currency address

external_id.description: A unique order_id or invoice_id from your store.

fiat.description: Currency code in ISO 4217 format for conversion. Currently, only USD is supported.

amount.description: The amount for which the invoice should be created in SHKeeper.

callback_url.description: The URL to which SHKeeper will send notifications in the event of transactions related to the created invoices.

amount.description: Amount in cryptocurrency with added SHKeeper fee

exchange_rate.description: cryptocurrency to fiat exchange rate for the moment of invoice generated

recalculate_after.description: Time to hold exchange rate for the client, if 0 - no recalculate when recived payment

wallet.description: Generated address for chosen cryptocurrency assigned to SHKeeper invoice

GetInvoiceByExternalIdResponse.description: Detailed information about SHKeeper invoice by external_id

GetAddressesByCryptoNameResponse.description: List of generated addresses for provided cryptocurrency

destination.description: For `XRP`, the destination address must be provided in the `X-address` format. The `X-address` format replaces the use of a separate destination tag when sending funds to a multi-user wallet on the `XRP` ledger, such as those of exchanges and custodial services.

fee.description:
Transaction fee.
`fee` must always be specified, even for cryptocurrencies with automatically determined fees.
For `BTC`, it is specified in `sat/vByte`; for `LTC` and `DOGE`, it is in `sat/Byte`.
For `XMR`, an integer (1-4) is passed to set the transaction priority:
`1` - Unimportant,
`2` - Normal,
`3` - Elevated,
`4` - Priority.
For other cryptocurrencies, any value can be passed, the optimal fee is calculated and set automatically, and this field is ignored.

dest_tag.description: Useful for `XRP` payout, for providing dest in a plain format, otherwise you should convert it to `X-address` format manually

CallbackPaymentRequest.description: Confirmed payment callback sent to the merchant callback_url. Current `build_payment_notification()` output contains invoice/payment facts and optional AML enrichment facts. It does not emit merchant-facing business decision fields; The consuming application makes credit/manual-review/reject decisions from the trigger transaction and AML facts.

external_id.description: Invoice or Order ID in the external system

addr.description: SHKeeper invoice wallet address that receives payments

fiat.description: Fiat currency (provided during payment request creation)

balance_fiat.description: SHKeeper invoice amount in fiat currency

paid.description: `true` if the payment request is fully paid, `false` if only a partial or no payment is received

overpaid_fiat.description: In case of SHKeeper invoice status `OVERPAID`, the overpaid amount will be shown here

fee_percent.description: SHKeeper fee percentage added to the invoice amount

fee_fixed.description: SHKeeper fixed fee in fiat added to the invoice amount

fee_policy.description: SHKeeper fee calculation policy. `PERCENT_OR_MINIMAL_FIXED_FEE` - use fixed fee value if percent from amount less than fixed

CallbackAmlPolicy.description: Local SHKeeper AML policy facts used when an AML check was skipped. These fields are informational; The consuming application decides whether to credit, review, or reject the deposit.

min_check_amount_fiat.description: Minimum fiat amount configured in SHKeeper before AML checking is required.

cumulative_amount_fiat.description: Cumulative fiat amount observed for the configured skip window.

cumulative_limit_fiat.description: Maximum cumulative fiat amount allowed by the local skip policy before AML checking is required.

CallbackAmlPayload.description: AML enrichment facts for the trigger transaction. SHKeeper does not emit business decision fields in this object. The consuming application must decide credit/manual review/reject from these facts and its own policy.

supported.description: `true` when the transaction asset/network is supported by the configured AML provider; `false` for unsupported assets such as networks not covered by Koinkyt.

checked.description: `true` only when SHKeeper received a usable AML result from the provider. `false` can mean unsupported, skipped by local policy, provider error, timeout, incomplete result, pending, or checking.

check_status.description: Normalized AML check status for consuming application integration. Use this field for application decisioning instead of raw `provider_status`.

reason_code.description: Normalized reason for unchecked or non-success AML state. `null` for successful checks and usually while a check is still pending/checking. Known values include `unsupported_asset`, `amount_below_shkeeper_threshold`, `aml_pending_timeout`, `aml_provider_error`, `incomplete_aml_result`, and raw provider/sidecar error codes.

provider_status.description: Raw AML provider/sidecar status. This is a source-system fact and may differ from normalized `check_status`, for example `provider_status=checking` with `check_status=error` after terminal retry failure.

score.description: AML risk score returned by the provider. SHKeeper serializes decimals as strings. `null` when no usable AML score is available.

asset.description: Asset passed to the AML provider, for example `USDT`.

network.description: Network/blockchain passed to the AML provider, for example `TRON`, `ETH`, or `BTC`.

signals.description: Provider-specific expanded AML signal payload. Koinkyt responses can include `from_entity`, `indirects`, `risk_score_grade`, `to_entity`, and `too_many_indirects`; exact shape depends on provider response.

error_code.description: Raw technical error code from SHKeeper/AML sidecar, if any. For business flow use normalized `reason_code`.

error_message.description: Raw technical error message from SHKeeper/AML sidecar, if any.

UnconfirmedCallbackPaymentRequest.description: Unconfirmed transaction callback sent by SHKeeper before final confirmation for networks that support unconfirmed notifications. This payload has a different shape from the confirmed payment callback and never includes AML enrichment.

external_id.description: Invoice or Order ID in the external system.

addr.description: Invoice wallet address that received the unconfirmed transaction.

external_id.description: Immutable consumer payout request id. It is unique inside the authenticated payout consumer and is used as the idempotency key.

asset.description: Payout execution asset. The current service contract supports USDT.

network.description: Blockchain rail for the USDT payout.

amount.description: Positive decimal string in USDT units. Accepted values are canonicalized to exactly 6 decimal places.

destination.description: Destination wallet address for the selected network.

status.description: Response marker. Submit returns ACCEPTED; status lookup returns OK.

state.description: Monotonic SHKeeper payout state. Known values include CREATED, PREFLIGHTED, ENQUEUEING, ENQUEUED, BROADCAST, CONFIRMED, FAILED_PRE_BROADCAST, FAILED_CHAIN_TERMINAL, RECONCILIATION_REQUIRED, MANUAL_REVIEW, SAFE_FOR_MANUAL_PAYOUT, MANUAL_PAYOUT_PENDING, and MANUAL_PAYOUT_COMPLETED.

resolution_status.description: Operator resolution action for an execution that needs review.

operator_note.description: Operator note stored with the audit row.

evidence.description: Structured technical evidence for the resolution. SHKeeper validates execution identity, last known state, source wallet, checked sources, chain search range, and action-specific tx or negative evidence. Business amount policies belong to the consuming application.

API_Key.description: SHKeeper supports user authentication and authorization through an API Key generated by the user. Send the API Key as a header value to Authorization with the format: `X-Shkeeper-Api-Key {API Key}`.
API_Key.name: X-Shkeeper-Api-Key

Basic.description: SHKeeper supports authenticating and authorizing users through the Basic HTTP authentication scheme. Send the user and password encoded in base64 with the format `Basic {base64(username:password)}`. Using this authentication is required for payout-related endpoints, and for metrics, but another credentials. Metric credentials can be set by environment variables: `METRICS_USERNAME`, `METRICS_PASSWORD`. The default `username`:`password` is `shkeeper`:`shkeeper`.

Payout_HMAC.description:
Service-to-service payout HMAC authentication. Send `X-Payout-Consumer`, `X-Payout-Key-Id`, `X-Payout-Timestamp`, `X-Payout-Nonce`, and `X-Payout-Signature`. The signature is HMAC-SHA256 over `timestamp
nonce
METHOD
canonical_path
canonical_query
body_sha256`.
Payout_HMAC.name: X-Payout-Signature

Basic_Optional.description: Optional Basic Auth: if `Authorization` header is provided, the user is attached to the request context (`g.user`). Otherwise, the request is treated as anonymous.

GetInvoiceByExternalIdResponseExample.summary: Get SHKeeper invoices status by external_id

GetAddressesByCryptoNameExample.summary: Get SHKeeper generated addresses by crypto_name

GetTransactionsByCryptoNameAndAddrRespon.summary: Get SHKeeper transactions for specified address of provided cryptocurrency

GetTransactionInfoByTxidAndExternalIdRes.summary: Get SHKeeper transactions for specified address of provided cryptocurrency

CallbackPaymentRequestExample.summary: Payment notification callback without AML enrichment
CallbackPaymentRequestExample.description: Confirmed callback without AML enrichment. Only the trigger transaction includes `aml` when AML enrichment is applicable or when the asset is explicitly unsupported.

CallbackPaymentRequestAmlCheckedExample.summary: Payment callback with successful AML enrichment
CallbackPaymentRequestAmlCheckedExample.description: Successful AML result. The consuming application receives the score and provider signals, then decides whether to credit, review, or reject according to its own policy.

CallbackPaymentRequestAmlUnsupportedExam.summary: Payment callback for asset not supported by AML provider
CallbackPaymentRequestAmlUnsupportedExam.description: Unsupported AML coverage is reported as unchecked AML facts. SHKeeper does not mark the deposit for manual review; The consuming application decides how to handle the payment.

aml.error_message: AML provider does not support this asset

CallbackPaymentRequestAmlSkippedExample.summary: Payment callback where AML check was skipped by local SHKeeper policy
CallbackPaymentRequestAmlSkippedExample.description: Small or cumulative-threshold-skipped transaction. The consuming application receives the skip policy facts and makes the final decision.

UnconfirmedCallbackPaymentRequestExample.description: Sent before final confirmation for supported networks. This callback has no AML object and should not be used as a final credit decision by the consuming application.

CryptoName.name: crypto_name
CryptoName.description: An identifier for cryptocurrency. Check [Get cryptocurrencies](#tag/Cryptos/operation/getCrypto) to get all available list.

ExternalId.name: external_id
ExternalId.description: An identifier order_id or invoice_id from your store.

tags.name: Payout callbacks
tags.description: Callbacks sent by SHKeeper after payout execution state changes.

post.description: Consumer webhook implemented by the upstream application. SHKeeper sends this signed callback whenever a payout execution changes state. Callback retries reuse `event_id` as `X-Payout-Nonce`; the consumer must deduplicate by `event_id` and apply events monotonically.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Consumer
parameters.description: Configured service consumer id for payout execution.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Key-Id
parameters.description: Configured key id for the payout HMAC secret.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp in seconds. Default allowed clock skew is 300 seconds.

parameters.name: X-Payout-Nonce
parameters.description: Submit/status requests must use a one-time nonce. Callback deliveries use the callback `event_id` as the nonce; retries for the same callback reuse that nonce and must be deduplicated by `event_id`.

400.description: Malformed callback body or stale/conflicting event

401.description: Missing or unknown payout auth headers

403.description: Invalid signature, expired timestamp, or invalid callback auth
